commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be healthy and flourishing; Well treated. Or affected. (Worthington) Bene patientes, eupathountes, "flourishing "(Grotius) tranquil, (St. Augustine) or in a prosperous condition. (Berthier) Erasmus, to show the utility of consulting the originals, informs us, what a multiplicity of authors he consulted in vain, to know the import of this word. (Amama) "They shall be fat and covered with leaves "(St. Jerome) alluding to the aforesaid comparison.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
